probes.name: add_multi_step_todo
probes.prompt: /todo add: I need to measure, project and 3D print a fixture to hold my bed frame that has broken. Also remind me to research money I should earn this quarter.
probes.criteria:
The /todo agent should add both task items via todo_manager.py, delegating categorisation to goals/task_categorizer (e.g. home-repair, finance). A good response confirms each item was added, states its category, and does not hallucinate extra context.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages #10 (3D print bed frame fixture) and #6 (research money to earn).

probes.name: list_all_todos
probes.prompt: /todo pokaż mi listę wszystkich moich zadań na todolist, both pending and completed ones
probes.criteria:
The /todo agent should call todo_manager.py to retrieve and display both pending and completed todos. A good response presents a clear, structured list (tables or numbered items) with status indicators for each task, using the Polish/English mix the user employed.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real message #16 (list tasks on todolist), with Polish phrasing consistent with the corpus mix.

probes.name: complete_and_verify
probes.prompt: /todo complete: the task about taxes. I already did my taxes today, so mark it done please.
probes.criteria:
The /todo agent should resolve 'the task about taxes' by searching existing todos, confirm the match, and mark it complete via todo_manager.py. A good response names the exact task it completed and confirms the status change rather than blindly completing without verification.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages #9 (thank you for reminding about taxes, will do today) and the completion flow.

probes.name: update_existing_todo
probes.prompt: Hey, I've already researched how much money I should earn, why is 'research money I should earn' still on my todo list as pending? Can you update it to done or remove it?
probes.criteria:
The /todo agent should find the specific todo item by matching the description, verify it exists, and either mark it complete or delete it via todo_manager.py. A good response identifies the exact matched task, states what action was taken, and does not ask for clarification when the intent is clear.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Directly inspired by real message #6 (already researched money, why is it still there).

probes.name: add_categorize_with_context
probes.prompt: /todo dodaj: przygotować tabelę z porównaniem prędkości dla różnych kart i współbieżności, plus fetch link do AI state-of-the-union video
probes.criteria:
The /todo agent should add both items via todo_manager.py, delegate categorisation to goals/task_categorizer (e.g. technical-benchmark, research), and confirm each addition with its assigned category. A good response handles the Polish request naturally, adds both tasks, and shows the categorisation result for each.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by real messages #27 (table of speeds for cards) and #28 (fetch AI state-of-the-union video), combining two distinct task types into one request.
